plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-369 RP12.P02.M04.S19-RP12.P02.M06.S14 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-369 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP12.P02 permission fixture slice: the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice test tail (happy/denied/review unit tests, integration smoke case), the full M05 Permission And Audit Binding service cycle (service entrypoint contract, request normalization, tenant boundary precheck, matter trace precheck, permission precheck, and the remaining service rows), and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (the first fourteen service rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H12), Claude review packet (C12), and CP00-370 handoff, without opening billing, proforma, invoice, or write-down runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary:
CP00-369 (Billing Core P02 Permission Fixture Slice, RP12.P02.M04.S19-RP12.P02.M06.S14, risk B, 40 units) is a clean descriptor-only pack with no blocking defects. Independent read-only inspection confirms full consistency across plan, registry, service, validators, test, contract, contract-validator script, README, manifest, command-evidence, and review baseline.

Verified: (1) The pack exists as a generated plan pack (closeout-pack-plan.json:412) with 40 included_units matching the baseline; live_state pins CP00-368 with next_subphase RP12.P02.M04.S19, consistent with CP369 being the pack under closeout. (2) Deliverable distribution {test:8,contract:2,implementation:17,security_audit:4,ui:5,claude_review:2,failure_recovery:2} and micro-title distribution {Secondary Workflow Slice:4, Permission And Audit Binding:22, Synthetic Fixture Set:14} match registry requirements and are enforced by validateBillingCoreCp369Coverage (validators.js:1077). (3) Descriptor-only / no-write / no-real-data boundaries: freezeCp369Result (service.js:687) pins ~40 runtime/write/leak guards to false; validateBillingCoreCp369P02PermissionFixtureSliceDescriptor (validators.js:1112) rejects any drift; manifest declares production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false. (4) Authority boundary preserved: claude_is_final_approval:false, claude_packet.read_only:true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false — enforced in descriptor, no_write_attestation, contract, and model.test.js. (5) CP178+ review hardening: complete invalid_review_blockers list present (failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, disabled_tools_run, missing_raw_output_reference, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_enterprise_trust_claim); readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON); review-request pins claude-opus-4-8/max/Read,Grep,Glob/read_only. (6) Hermes patterns used only for deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false), not product runtime. (7) Range continuity holds: CP368 (last RP12.P02.M04.S18, next S19) -> CP369 (first S19, last RP12.P02.M06.S14, next S15) -> CP370, with historical_pack_bindings ending at CP00-369.

The pack-level production_ready claim is appropriately false (status implemented_pending_review / implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout); the only positive readiness signal, production_ready_candidate, is correctly bounded to coverage.valid && slice.valid. No over-claim of enterprise trust or final approval. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, raw-output.json, review-receipt.json) are expectedly absent pre-closeout since they are produced from this review run and downstream adjudication; their absence is not a defect at review time. Unrelated dirty files (LDIP invalid-attempt JSON, workbook specs, docx) are correctly classified out of pack scope and were not treated as pack content. Claude remained a read-only independent reviewer and is not the final approval authority; human final approval for any enterprise-trust/runtime-opening claim remains required.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-369 billing core p02 permission fixture slice descriptor pack
